sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, the player is now to the right of the left frame and below the center frame. The player is at col 24-27 (approximately), and the center-below frame is at col 32-35. Let me check the segmentation to see the exact positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Player position:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

print("\nFrames:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'G']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player is at row 37-39 (green body), col 28-31. The white head is at row 32-36, col 28-31. The center-below frame is at row 36-39, col 32-35. So the player is to the left of the center-below frame, and slightly above it (the green body is at row 37-39, and the frame is at row 36-39).
Let me move RIGHT to be below the center-below frame, then try pressing SPACE.
Actually, the player's green body is at row 37-39, and the frame is at row 36-39. So the player is at the same row range as the frame, just to the left. Let me move RIGHT to be below the frame.
Wait, the frame is at row 36-39, col 32-35. The player is at row 37-39, col 28-31. So the player is to the left of the frame, at the same row range.
To be below the frame, I need to move RIGHT to col 32-35 and then DOWN to row 40-43. But the frame is at row 36-39, so I need to be at row 40-43 to be below it.
Actually, let me first move RIGHT to be in the same column range as the frame (col 32-35), then try pressing SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move RIGHT to be in the same column range as the center-below frame
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
